1370	\begin{lemma}
1371	
1372	\leanok
1373	[The section-level base-change map is the base change of the unit]
1374	  \label{lem:base_change_mate_section_identity}
1375	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_section_identity}
1376	  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map, lem:base_change_mate_domain_read,
1377	        lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read, lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}
1378	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
1379	  % `lemma-affine-base-change' (\S\,Cohomology and base change, I), proof, the
1380	  % "boils down to the equality" step (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
1381	  % L932--937).
1382	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Thus we see that the lemma boils down to the
1383	  % equality
1384	  % $$
1385	  % (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M = R' \otimes_R M
1386	  % $$
1387	  % as $R'$-modules."
1388	  % SOURCE (cohomological form): [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Tag 02KH
1389	  % `lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology', part (2) (read from
1390	  % references/stacks-coherent.tex, L967--968) — the H^0/direct-image-with-base-change
1391	  % specialisation this affine identity underlies.
1392	  % SOURCE QUOTE (cohomological form): "if $S = \Spec(A)$ and $S' = \Spec(B)$, then
1393	  % $H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B = H^i(X', \mathcal{F}')$."
1394	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma ``Affine base
1395	  change'' (proof, ``boils down to the equality'' step); cohomological form Tag 02KH(2).}
1396	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (the scaffold creates the decl pinned above with this signature).
1397	  %   The conjugate of `Γ(θ)` through the domain read `Θ_src` and codomain read `Θ_tgt`
1398	  %   is the `R'`-base change of the algebraic unit `η_M : M → (A ⊗_R R') ⊗_A M`,
1399	  %   `m ↦ (1 ⊗ₜ 1) ⊗ₜ m` (`LinearMap.lTensor R' η_M` post-composed with the `R'`-action),
1400	  %   which on the generator `r' ⊗ m` returns `r' • ((1 ⊗ 1) ⊗ m) = (1 ⊗ r') ⊗ m`; this is
1401	  %   exactly `(base_change_mate_regroupEquiv ψ φ M).inv`. The formalized conclusion is the
1402	  %   propositional equality of `ModuleCat R'` morphisms:
1403	  %     {R R' A : CommRingCat.{u}} (ψ : R ⟶ R') (φ : R ⟶ A) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :
1404	  %       (base_change_mate_domain_read ψ φ M).inv ≫
1405	  %         (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R')).map
1406	  %           (pushforwardBaseChangeMap (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)
1407	  %             (Limits.pullback.snd (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))
1408	  %             (Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))
1409	  %             (IsPullback.of_hasPullback (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)).w (tilde M)) ≫
1410	  %         (base_change_mate_codomain_read ψ φ M).hom
1411	  %         = (base_change_mate_regroupEquiv ψ φ M).inv
1412	  In the affine--affine model (\(g = \operatorname{Spec}\psi\),
1413	  \(f = \operatorname{Spec}\varphi\), \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde M\) for an \(A\)-module
1414	  \(M\)), write \(\Gamma(\theta)\) for the section-level incarnation of the base-change
1415	  map \texttt{pushforwardBaseChangeMap}
1416	  (Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map}). Conjugate it through the two reads:
1417	  the domain read \(\Theta_{\mathrm{src}}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_domain_read})
1418	  identifies its source \(\Gamma\bigl(g^*(f_*\widetilde M)\bigr)\) with \(R' \otimes_R M\),
1419	  and the codomain read \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\)
1420	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}) identifies its target
1421	  \(\Gamma\bigl(f'_*(g')^*\widetilde M\bigr)\) with \((A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\). Then
1422	  the conjugated map
1423	  \[
1424	    \Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}} \circ \Gamma(\theta) \circ \Theta_{\mathrm{src}}^{-1} :
1425	    R' \otimes_R M \longrightarrow (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M
1426	  \]
1427	  is the \(R'\)-base change of the algebraic unit of extension of scalars along
1428	  \(A \to A \otimes_R R'\), namely \(\eta_M : M \to (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\),
1429	  \(m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\): explicitly it is the unique \(R'\)-linear map
1430	  sending \(r' \otimes m \mapsto r' \cdot \eta_M(m) = (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\)
1431	  (i.e.\ \(\operatorname{LinearMap.lTensor} R'\,\eta_M\) followed by the \(R'\)-action on
1432	  the target). Equivalently, this conjugated map is the \emph{inverse} of the regrouping
1433	  isomorphism Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv},
1434	  \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}} \circ \Gamma(\theta) \circ \Theta_{\mathrm{src}}^{-1}
1435	  = \operatorname{regroup}^{-1}\), and in particular an isomorphism with \emph{no}
1436	  flatness hypothesis.
1437	\end{lemma}
1438	
1439	\begin{proof}
1440	  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map, lem:base_change_mate_domain_read,
1441	        lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read, lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}
1442	  The argument is carried out \emph{directly on the module of global sections}; no
1443	  abstract adjoint-mate identification at the level of sheaves intervenes. By
1444	  hom-extensionality for \(R'\)-linear maps it suffices to check the asserted equality on
1445	  the \(R'\)-module generators \(r' \otimes m\) of the source \(R' \otimes_R M\), and by
1446	  \(R'\)-linearity on the generators \(1 \otimes m\).
1447	
1448	  \emph{The base-change map is the base change of the unit.} For a quasi-coherent sheaf
1449	  on an affine scheme the base-change comparison \(\theta\) is, by construction, the
1450	  pullback–pushforward comparison obtained by applying \(g^*\) to the unit of the
1451	  \(\bigl((g')^*, (g')_*\bigr)\)-adjunction; read on global sections through the two tilde
1452	  dictionaries this is precisely the extension of scalars along \(\psi : R \to R'\) of the
1453	  algebraic unit of extension of scalars along \(A \to A \otimes_R R'\). Concretely, the
1454	  unit \(\eta_M : M \to (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\) sends \(m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1)
1455	  \otimes m\) (the codomain read identifies \(\Gamma\bigl(f'_*(g')^*\widetilde M\bigr)\)
1456	  with \((A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\), Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}),
1457	  and the domain read identifies the source with \(R' \otimes_R M\)
1458	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_domain_read}). The base-change map therefore is the
1459	  \(R'\)-linearization \(\operatorname{LinearMap.lTensor} R'\,\eta_M\) of \(\eta_M\)
1460	  evaluated through the \(R'\)-action on the target: on the generator \(r' \otimes m\),
1461	  \[
1462	    r' \otimes m \;\longmapsto\; r' \cdot \eta_M(m)
1463	    \;=\; r' \cdot \bigl((1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\bigr)
1464	    \;=\; (1 \otimes r') \otimes m ,
1465	  \]
1466	  using that the \(R'\)-action on \((A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\) is restriction of
1467	  scalars along \(R' \to A \otimes_R R'\), \(r' \mapsto 1 \otimes r'\).
1468	
1469	  \emph{Identification with \(\operatorname{regroup}^{-1}\).} The regrouping isomorphism
1470	  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv} has inverse sending
1471	  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\) on generators. This is the same
1472	  assignment as the displayed map above, and both maps are \(R'\)-linear, so they
1473	  coincide. This is exactly the ``boils down to the equality''
1474	  \((A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M = R' \otimes_R M\) of the source, read as an identity of
1475	  \(R'\)-modules; the regrouping isomorphism realizes the equality and the base-change map
1476	  is its inverse. In particular the conjugated map is an isomorphism, with no flatness
1477	  hypothesis used anywhere.
1478	\end{proof}

1524	  % NOTE (iter-002): the Lean decl formalizes the `IsIso (Γ(α))` *corollary* of

1793	\begin{lemma}[Flat base change preserves finite equalizers]
1794	  \label{lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib}
1795	  \lean{LinearMap.tensorEqLocusEquiv}
1796	  \mathlibok
1797	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
1798	  Let \(A \to B\) be a flat ring homomorphism (more generally, let \(B\) be a flat
1799	  \(A\)-module that is also an \(A\)-algebra, with the scalar-tower compatibility),
1800	  and let \(f, g : N \rightrightarrows P\) be a pair of \(A\)-linear maps. Write
1801	  \(\operatorname{eq}(f, g) = \{x \in N : f(x) = g(x)\}\) for their equalizer. Then the
1802	  canonical \(B\)-linear comparison map
1803	  \[
1804	    B \otimes_A \operatorname{eq}(f, g)
1805	    \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
1806	    \operatorname{eq}\bigl(\mathbf{1}_B \otimes f,\ \mathbf{1}_B \otimes g\bigr)
1807	  \]
1808	  is an isomorphism; that is, base change along a flat algebra commutes with the
1809	  formation of the finite equalizer (equivalently, of the kernel: the case
1810	  \(g = 0\) is \texttt{LinearMap.tensorKerEquiv}, underlain by
1811	  \texttt{Module.Flat.ker\_lTensor\_eq} and \texttt{Module.Flat.eqLocus\_lTensor\_eq}).
1812	  Since \(- \otimes_A B\) also preserves finite products, it follows that
1813	  \(- \otimes_A B\) carries the degree-\(0\)/\(1\) equalizer
1814	  \(\prod_i \Gamma(U_i, \mathcal{F}) \rightrightarrows
1815	    \prod_{i,j} \Gamma(U_{ij}, \mathcal{F})\) of a finite cover to the corresponding
1816	  equalizer of the base-changed cover.
1817	\end{lemma}

1897	% $H^i(X', \mathcal{F}')$. Since pullback by $g$ corresponds
1898	% to $- \otimes_A B$ on modules
1899	% (Schemes, Lemma \ref{schemes-lemma-widetilde-pullback})
1900	% we see that it suffices to prove (2).
1901	%
1902	% Let $A \to B$ be a flat ring homomorphism.
1903	% Let $X$ be a quasi-compact and quasi-separated scheme over $A$.
1904	% Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
1905	% Set $X_B = X \times_{\Spec(A)} \Spec(B)$ and denote
1906	% $\mathcal{F}_B$ the pullback of $\mathcal{F}$.
1907	% We are trying to show that the map
1908	% $$
1909	% H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \longrightarrow H^i(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B)
1910	% $$
1911	% (given by the reference in the statement of the lemma)
1912	% is an isomorphism.
1913	%
1914	% In case $X$ is separated, choose an affine open covering
1915	% $\mathcal{U} : X = U_1 \cup \ldots \cup U_t$ and recall that
1916	% $$
1917	% \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = H^p(X, \mathcal{F}),
1918	% $$
1919	% see
1920	% Lemma \ref{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent}.
1921	% If $\mathcal{U}_B : X_B = (U_1)_B \cup \ldots \cup (U_t)_B$ we obtain
1922	% by base change, then it is still the case that each $(U_i)_B$ is affine
1923	% and that $X_B$ is separated. Thus we obtain
1924	% $$
1925	% \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}_B, \mathcal{F}_B) = H^p(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B).
1926	% $$
1927	% We have the following relation between the {\v C}ech complexes
1928	% $$
1929	% \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}_B, \mathcal{F}_B) =
1930	% \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B
1931	% $$
1932	% as follows from
1933	% Lemma \ref{lemma-affine-base-change}.
1934	% Since $A \to B$ is flat, the same thing remains true on taking cohomology.
1935	%
1936	% In case $X$ is quasi-separated, choose an affine open covering
1937	% $\mathcal{U} : X = U_1 \cup \ldots \cup U_t$. We will use the
1938	% {\v C}ech-to-cohomology spectral sequence
1939	% Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-cech-spectral-sequence}.
1940	% The reader who wishes to avoid this spectral sequence
1941	% can use Mayer-Vietoris and induction on $t$ as in the proof of
1942	% Lemma \ref{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images}."
1943	% NOTE: For the $i = 0$ case the argument is Čech-free: $H^0(X,\mathcal{F})$ is the
1944	% sheaf-condition equalizer of a finite affine cover (Čech degree 0/1, NOT Čech
1945	% cohomology -- no Leray, no spectral sequence), each term base-changes by the affine
1946	% lemma lem:affine_base_change_pushforward, and flatness of $A \to B$ commutes
1947	% $-\otimes_A B$ with that finite equalizer via lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib
1948	% (Mathlib's LinearMap.tensorEqLocusEquiv). The quasi-separated case is the
1949	% Mayer--Vietoris induction on the number of cover members named in the source quote.
1950	\begin{proof}
1951	  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map, lem:affine_base_change_pushforward, lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib}
1952	  For \(i = 0\) the argument is {\v C}ech-\emph{free}: it uses only the sheaf
1953	  condition (the degree-\(0\)/\(1\) part of {\v C}ech, i.e.\ an equalizer of a finite
1954	  cover), the affine lemma, and the fact that flat base change commutes with that
1955	  finite equalizer. There is no Leray sequence and no {\v C}ech-to-cohomology spectral
1956	  sequence.
1957	
1958	  \medskip
1959	  \emph{Reduction to the affine target.} The statement is local on \(S'\), so we
1960	  may assume \(S\) and \(S'\) are affine, say \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) and
1961	  \(S' = \operatorname{Spec}(B)\) with \(A \to B\) flat. Because \(f\) is
1962	  quasi-compact and quasi-separated, \(f_*\mathcal{F}\) is quasi-coherent on
1963	  \(S\); it is therefore the sheaf associated to the \(A\)-module
1964	  \(H^0(S, f_*\mathcal{F}) = H^0(X, \mathcal{F}) = \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})\). Likewise
1965	  \(f'_*\mathcal{F}'\) is the sheaf on \(S'\) associated to the \(B\)-module
1966	  \(H^0(X', \mathcal{F}') = \Gamma(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B)\). Since pullback along \(g\)
1967	  corresponds to \(- \otimes_A B\) on modules, the base-change map becomes the
1968	  comparison map of \(B\)-modules
1969	  \[
1970	    \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \longrightarrow \Gamma(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B),
1971	    \qquad X_B = X \times_{\operatorname{Spec}(A)} \operatorname{Spec}(B),
1972	  \]
1973	  and it suffices to prove that this map is an isomorphism.
1974	
1975	  \medskip
1976	  \emph{\(H^0\) as a finite equalizer (the sheaf condition).} Choose a finite affine
1977	  open cover \(\mathcal{U} : X = U_1 \cup \dots \cup U_t\) (possible since \(X\) is
1978	  quasi-compact). The sheaf condition for \(\mathcal{F}\) on this cover presents the
1979	  global sections as the equalizer of the two restriction maps
1980	  \[
1981	    \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})
1982	    \;=\;
1983	    \operatorname{eq}\Bigl(
1984	      \textstyle\prod_i \Gamma(U_i, \mathcal{F})
1985	      \;\rightrightarrows\;
1986	      \textstyle\prod_{i,j} \Gamma(U_{ij}, \mathcal{F})
1987	    \Bigr),
1988	    \qquad U_{ij} = U_i \cap U_j,
1989	  \]
1990	  where the two parallel maps are \(s \mapsto (s_i|_{U_{ij}})\) and
1991	  \(s \mapsto (s_j|_{U_{ij}})\). This is precisely Čech degree \(0\) into degree
1992	  \(1\); it is the sheaf axiom and requires \emph{no} separatedness of \(X\) and no
1993	  higher cohomology.
1994	
1995	  \medskip
1996	  \emph{The separated case.} Suppose first that \(X\) is separated over \(A\). Then
1997	  every intersection \(U_{ij}\) is affine. Base changing the cover yields a finite
1998	  affine cover \(\mathcal{U}_B : X_B = (U_1)_B \cup \dots \cup (U_t)_B\) of \(X_B\),
1999	  and the same sheaf-condition equalizer presents \(\Gamma(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B)\). The
2000	  affine lemma Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_base_change_pushforward} (in its
2001	  \(i = 0\)/global-sections form) identifies each base-changed term with the original
2002	  term tensored over \(A\) with \(B\):
2003	  \[
2004	    \Gamma\bigl((U_i)_B, \mathcal{F}_B\bigr) \cong \Gamma(U_i, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B,
2005	    \qquad
2006	    \Gamma\bigl((U_{ij})_B, \mathcal{F}_B\bigr) \cong \Gamma(U_{ij}, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B,
2007	  \]
2008	  compatibly with the two restriction maps. Hence the equalizer diagram for \(X_B\) is
2009	  the diagram for \(X\) with \(- \otimes_A B\) applied (using that \(- \otimes_A B\)
2010	  commutes with the finite products \(\prod_i\), \(\prod_{i,j}\)). Because \(A \to B\)
2011	  is flat, base change commutes with the formation of this finite equalizer
2012	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib}, i.e.\ Mathlib's
2013	  \texttt{LinearMap.tensorEqLocusEquiv}):
2014	  \[
2015	    \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B
2016	    = \operatorname{eq}(\cdots) \otimes_A B
2017	    \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
2018	    \operatorname{eq}(\cdots \otimes_A B)
2019	    = \Gamma(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B).
2020	  \]
2021	  This is exactly the comparison map of the reduction step, so it is an isomorphism.
2022	
2023	  \medskip
2024	  \emph{The quasi-separated case.} In general \(X\) need only be quasi-separated. We
2025	  argue by Mayer--Vietoris induction on the number \(t\) of members of a finite affine
2026	  cover \(\mathcal{U} : X = U_1 \cup \dots \cup U_t\), each step reducing to the
2027	  separated/affine case. For \(t = 1\), \(X = U_1\) is affine and the claim is the
2028	  affine lemma. For \(t > 1\), set \(V = U_t\) (affine) and
2029	  \(W = U_1 \cup \dots \cup U_{t-1}\) (an open covered by \(t - 1\) affines). Their
2030	  intersection \(W \cap V = \bigcup_{i < t}(U_i \cap U_t)\) is covered by the
2031	  \(t - 1\) opens \(U_i \cap U_t\), each of which is quasi-compact and separated
2032	  (an open of the affine, hence separated, \(U_i\)); thus \(W \cap V\) is separated
2033	  and quasi-compact, and flat base change holds for it by the separated case. The
2034	  two-member cover \(\{W, V\}\) of \(X\) gives the left-exact Mayer--Vietoris
2035	  sequence — itself the sheaf-condition equalizer for that cover —
2036	  \[
2037	    0 \longrightarrow \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})
2038	      \longrightarrow \Gamma(W, \mathcal{F}) \oplus \Gamma(V, \mathcal{F})
2039	      \longrightarrow \Gamma(W \cap V, \mathcal{F}),
2040	  \]
2041	  and likewise for \(X_B\) with \(\{W_B, V_B\}\). Flatness of \(A \to B\) makes
2042	  \(- \otimes_A B\) preserve this finite kernel/equalizer
2043	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib}); since flat base change holds for
2044	  \(V\) (affine), for \(W\) (inductive hypothesis, \(t - 1\) members), and for
2045	  \(W \cap V\) (separated), it follows for \(X\). In degree \(0\) this gives
2046	  \(\Gamma(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \cong \Gamma(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B)\), as required.
2047	\end{proof}

2	\label{chap:Cohomology_RegroupHelper}
3	% This chapter blueprints the standalone, geometry-free helper that lives in
4	% AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/RegroupHelper.lean (1:1 slug). It is the pure

9	% that blocks the same-file construction).

11	\section{The R'-linear regrouping equivalence}
12	
13	\begin{lemma}
14	
15	\leanok
16	[Regrouping linear equivalence for the tensor tower]
17	  \label{lem:base_change_regroup_linearEquiv}
18	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_regroup_linearEquiv}
19	  \uses{lem:isPushout_cancelBaseChange_mathlib}
20	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
21	  % `lemma-affine-base-change' (\S\,Cohomology and base change, I), proof, the
22	  % "boils down to the equality" step (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
23	  % L933--938).
24	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Thus we see that the lemma boils down to the
25	  % equality
26	  % $$
27	  % (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M = R' \otimes_R M
28	  % $$
29	  % as $R'$-modules."
30	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma ``Affine base
31	  change'' (proof, ``boils down to the equality'' step).}
32	  Let \(\psi : R \to R'\) and \(\varphi : R \to A\) be ring homomorphisms and \(M\)
33	  an \(A\)-module. On the \emph{canonical} Mathlib instances (with \(R'\) acting on
34	  \(A \otimes_R R'\) through the \(\texttt{Algebra.TensorProduct.rightAlgebra}\)
35	  factor, i.e.\ \(r' \cdot (a \otimes s) = a \otimes (r' s)\)) there is a bundled
36	  \(R'\)-linear isomorphism
37	  \[
38	    \operatorname{regroup} :
39	    (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; R' \otimes_R M ,
40	  \]
41	  the ``equality as \(R'\)-modules'' of the affine base-change source. On generators
42	  it sends \((a \otimes r') \otimes m \mapsto r' \otimes (a \cdot m)\); in
43	  particular \((1 \otimes r') \otimes m \mapsto r' \otimes m\), with inverse
44	  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\). It carries \emph{no} flatness
45	  hypothesis.
46	\end{lemma}
47	
48	\begin{proof}
49	  \uses{lem:isPushout_cancelBaseChange_mathlib}
50	  \leanok
51	  This is pure tensor algebra, and it is supplied \emph{directly} by Mathlib's
52	  pushout-form base-change cancellation
53	  \(\operatorname{Algebra.IsPushout.cancelBaseChange}\)
54	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:isPushout_cancelBaseChange_mathlib}), instantiated at \(S = R'\),
55	  \(B = A \otimes_R R'\):
56	  \[
57	    \operatorname{cancelBaseChange} R\,R'\,A\,(A \otimes_R R')\,M :
58	    (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; R' \otimes_R M .
59	  \]
60	  The required pushout instance \(\operatorname{Algebra.IsPushout} R\,R'\,A\,
61	  (A \otimes_R R')\) is supplied for free by \(\operatorname{TensorProduct.isPushout'}\).
62	  The decisive structural point is that this equivalence is \emph{natively}
63	  \(R'\)-linear: the cancellation is bundled as a \(\operatorname{LinearEquiv}\) over
64	  \(R'\) (its base-ring slot is \(S = R'\)), so the \(R'\)-linearity is part of the
65	  Mathlib datum with no hand-written scalar-compatibility obligation.
66	  On the generator it computes, by \(\texttt{cancelBaseChange\_tmul}\),
67	  \[
68	    (a \otimes r') \otimes m \;\longmapsto\; r' \otimes (a \cdot m) ,
69	  \]
70	  with inverse \(r' \otimes m \mapsto (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\)
71	  (\(\texttt{cancelBaseChange\_symm\_tmul}\)); the \(R'\)-action enters through the
72	  \(A \otimes_R R'\) factor (\(r' \cdot (a \otimes s) = a \otimes (r' s)\)). Being a
73	  single bundled Mathlib linear equivalence it is an \(R'\)-linear isomorphism, and it
74	  carries no flatness hypothesis.
75	
76	\end{proof}
77

10	
11	This file contains `AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_regroup_linearEquiv`, the
12	axiom-clean `R'`-linear equivalence `(A ⊗[R] R') ⊗[A] M ≃ₗ[R'] R' ⊗[R] M`

33	/-! ## Regrouping isomorphism for the section-level mate (L4-a) -/
34	
35	/-- **Regrouping isomorphism, pure-tensor-algebra core.** For ring homomorphisms `ψ : R → R'`,
36	`φ : R → A` (giving `A` the `R`-algebra structure) and an `A`-module `M` (with `M` an `R`-module
37	via `φ` and `IsScalarTower R A M`), there is a bundled `R'`-linear isomorphism
38	\[ (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; R' \otimes_R M, \]
39	where `R'` acts on the source through the `right`-algebra structure on `A ⊗[R] R'`
40	(`Algebra.TensorProduct.rightAlgebra`, i.e. the `includeRight` factor). On the generator it sends
41	`(1 ⊗ r') ⊗ m ↦ r' ⊗ m`; its inverse sends `r' ⊗ m ↦ (1 ⊗ r') ⊗ m`. It carries **no** flatness
42	hypothesis.
43	
44	The construction is exactly the blueprint composite
45	`comm ≪≫ cancelBaseChange ≪≫ comm` (in the `A ⊗_R R'` orientation):
46	\[ (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M \xrightarrow{\texttt{comm}} M \otimes_A (A \otimes_R R')
47	   \xrightarrow{\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}} M \otimes_R R'
48	   \xrightarrow{\texttt{comm}} R' \otimes_R M, \]
49	assembled at the additive level from Mathlib's `TensorProduct.comm` (×2) and
50	`TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange` (instantiated at the tower `R → A → A` with
51	module `M` and base-change object `R'`), then re-bundled as an `R'`-linear equivalence by verifying
52	`R'`-`map_smul` on generators (`cancelBaseChange_tmul` plus the `rightAlgebra` action
53	`r' • (a ⊗ s) = a ⊗ (r' * s)`). This is the **mathematical heart** of the section-level mate
54	identification (`lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv`); the object-level packaging needed by the
55	chain is `base_change_mate_regroupEquiv` below. Axiom-clean (`propext`, `Quot.sound`).
56	
57	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma "Affine base change", proof, the
58	"boils down to the equality `(R' ⊗_R A) ⊗_A M = R' ⊗_R M`" step. -/
59	noncomputable def base_change_regroup_linearEquiv
60	    {R A R' : Type u} [CommRing R] [CommRing A] [CommRing R']

59	noncomputable def base_change_regroup_linearEquiv
60	    {R A R' : Type u} [CommRing R] [CommRing A] [CommRing R']
61	    [Algebra R A] [Algebra R R'] (M : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [Module A M]
62	    [IsScalarTower R A M] :
63	    letI : Algebra R' (A ⊗[R] R') := Algebra.TensorProduct.rightAlgebra
64	    ((A ⊗[R] R') ⊗[A] M) ≃ₗ[R'] (R' ⊗[R] M) := by
65	  letI : Algebra R' (A ⊗[R] R') := Algebra.TensorProduct.rightAlgebra
66	  -- The underlying additive composite `comm ≫ cancelBaseChange ≫ comm`.
67	  let g : ((A ⊗[R] R') ⊗[A] M) ≃+ (R' ⊗[R] M) :=
68	    (TensorProduct.comm A (A ⊗[R] R') M).toAddEquiv.trans
69	      (((TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange R A A M R').toAddEquiv).trans
70	        (TensorProduct.comm R M R').toAddEquiv)
71	  refine { g with map_smul' := ?_ }
72	  intro r' x
73	  simp only [AddEquiv.toFun_eq_coe, RingHom.id_apply]
74	  induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
75	  | zero => simp
76	  | add a b ha hb => rw [smul_add, map_add, map_add, smul_add, ha, hb]
77	  | tmul t m =>
78	    induction t using TensorProduct.induction_on with
79	    | zero => simp
80	    | add a b ha hb => rw [TensorProduct.add_tmul, smul_add, map_add, map_add, smul_add, ha, hb]
81	    | tmul a s =>
82	      -- `r' • ((a ⊗ s) ⊗ m) = (a ⊗ (r' * s)) ⊗ m` via the `rightAlgebra` action on `A ⊗[R] R'`.
83	      have hsmul : r' • ((a ⊗ₜ[R] s) ⊗ₜ[A] m) = (a ⊗ₜ[R] (r' * s)) ⊗ₜ[A] m := by
84	        rw [TensorProduct.smul_tmul']
85	        congr 1
86	        rw [Algebra.smul_def, show (algebraMap R' (A ⊗[R] R')) r' = (1 : A) ⊗ₜ[R] r' from rfl,
87	          Algebra.TensorProduct.tmul_mul_tmul]
88	        simp
89	      rw [hsmul]
90	      change (TensorProduct.comm R M R') (TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange
91	          R A A M R' ((TensorProduct.comm A (A ⊗[R] R') M) ((a ⊗ₜ[R] (r' * s)) ⊗ₜ[A] m)))
92	        = r' • g ((a ⊗ₜ[R] s) ⊗ₜ[A] m)
93	      show _ = r' • (TensorProduct.comm R M R') (TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange
94	          R A A M R' ((TensorProduct.comm A (A ⊗[R] R') M) ((a ⊗ₜ[R] s) ⊗ₜ[A] m)))
95	      simp only [TensorProduct.comm_tmul, TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange_tmul,
96	        TensorProduct.smul_tmul']
97	      rw [smul_eq_mul]
98	
99	end AlgebraicGeometry
